evals.prompt: I just finished implementing user authentication with JWT tokens. There are changes in auth/service.go, auth/service_test.go, and I also fixed a typo in README.md while I was at it. Can you commit everything properly?
evals.expected_output: Agent separates the auth feature (service + test) into one feat commit and the README typo fix into a separate docs commit, each with a validated single-line message
expectations: Creates at least 2 separate commits (auth changes and docs change)
Auth commit uses feat type and groups service.go with service_test.go
README commit uses docs type
Each commit message follows type: description format without scope
Validation script is run before each commit

evals.prompt: I have a big service.go file where I refactored the error handling (renamed variables, extracted a helper) and also added a new endpoint for user deletion. These are mixed in the same file. Please commit them separately.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses the staging tool to parse hunks, stages refactoring hunks separately from the new endpoint hunks, creates two commits: one refactor and one feat
expectations: Uses the staging tool (git-staging.pyz) parse mode to identify hunks
Creates separate commits for refactor and behavioral change
Refactor commit uses refactor type
New endpoint commit uses feat type
Does not use git add -p (uses staging tool instead)

evals.expected_output: Agent autonomously analyzes the working tree, determines the commit strategy based on semantic diff analysis, validates messages, and creates appropriate commits without asking unnecessary questions
expectations: Runs git status, git diff HEAD, git diff --cached, and git log
Performs semantic diff analysis before committing
Validates each message with validate-commit.py before git commit
Reports final result with commits created and status

evals.expected_output: Agent detects clean working directory and reports it without attempting any commits
evals.setup_note: Run this eval on a clean repo with no uncommitted changes
expectations: Runs git status and git diff HEAD
Detects no changes to commit
Reports clean working directory
Does not attempt to create any commits
Does not ask unnecessary questions

evals.prompt: I forgot to add the config file to my last commit. Can you amend it to include config/settings.yaml?
evals.expected_output: Agent stages config/settings.yaml and amends the last commit, reusing or updating the message after validation
expectations: Stages config/settings.yaml
Uses git commit --amend
Validates the commit message before amending
Does not create a new separate commit
Reports the amended commit

evals.prompt: commit the changes in the binary image file logo.png and the text change in README.md
evals.expected_output: Agent handles binary file gracefully, commits both changes with appropriate type
expectations: Recognizes logo.png as binary
Stages both files appropriately
Does not attempt to compute hunks for binary file
Creates commit with validated message
Uses appropriate commit type (chore or docs depending on grouping)

evals.expected_output: Agent uses git-staging.pyz commit subcommand to stage and commit in one step, after validating the message first
expectations: Uses git-staging.pyz commit mode (not just stage mode)
Validates message with validate-commit.py BEFORE calling commit mode
Does not call git commit separately after staging tool commit
Reports the commit result

evals.prompt: /commit unstage the payment changes and only commit the auth changes
evals.expected_output: Agent uses unstage capability to remove payment changes from staging, then commits only auth changes
expectations: Uses staging tool unstage mode or git reset for payment files
Only auth-related changes are in the final commit
Validates message before committing
Reports what was committed and what was left unstaged
